fingerprint: SAA multi-station decay relies entirely on INTERMAGNET + CHAOS-7 globe infrastructure; Domain 8 uses pred=obs identity check (77.0/77.0); 28 nT/yr labeled 'global network average' in parameters.json but is an SAA regional value; FORM-111 references nonexistent independent_verification.py; dome's axisymmetric H(r) cannot produce the inter-cell boundary interpretation claimed in wins.html
globe_value: SAA decay rates computed from IGRF-13/CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic models. Spatial structure (two-cell morphology, inter-cell boundary, longitude-dependent decay rates) derived from Gauss coefficients in spherical harmonic expansion — a decomposition that only exists on a sphere.
dome_value: field_decay_rate_nT_yr = 28 in inject_ai_layer.py (hardcoded core parameter). Domain 8: pred=77.0, obs=77.0 (identity check). parameters.json: field_decay_rate = 28 nT/yr described as 'Global network average'. No dome derivation of any of these values.
match_precision: N/A — not a single-constant fingerprint but a structural dependency on globe geomagnetic infrastructure
why_it_matters:
Five independent globe fingerprints converge on WIN-041:

1. PRED=OBS IDENTITY PATTERN: Domain 8 in monitor.py sets predicted=77.0 and observed=77.0 with tolerance_pct=20.0. The predicted value is not computed from dome geometry — it is copied from the observation. This is the same pred=obs identity pattern found in 3+ other monitoring domains. The dome's 'monitoring' of SAA decay is not a test of the model; it is a check that the observation still equals itself.

2. MISLABELED PARAMETER: parameters.json declares field_decay_rate=28 nT/yr with derivation 'Global network average.' But 28 nT/yr is NOT a global average — the global mean secular variation is ~5-10 nT/yr at most stations outside the SAA. The value 28 nT/yr is a regional SAA observation, not a global average. Labeling it 'global' disguises its origin as a regional SAA measurement. The dome set its threshold to match the region it plans to test, guaranteeing confirmation.

3. PHANTOM SOURCE FILE: FORM-111 ('Find SAA Nodes') in formulas.json references source file 'independent_verification.py.' This file does not exist in the repository (HTTP 404). The formula claims to track multi-station SAA node coordinates from CHAOS-7 data, but the code that would compute these values is missing. This is the same phantom-file pattern found in WIN-020 (dome_predictor_interactive.py and v19_pipeline.py also don't exist). The dome references nonexistent computational infrastructure to create the appearance of derivation.

4. SPHERICAL HARMONIC DATA DEPENDENCY: All five station decay rates cited in wins.html (TDC −79.7, SHE −54.2, HER −45.3, PST −38.8, KOU) come from INTERMAGNET observatories whose data is processed through IGRF/CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic models. The dome's exponential decay models (FORM-015, FORM-048, FORM-098, FORM-106) are all empirical curve-fits to globe-model outputs — exponentials fitted to CHAOS-7 time series using I0·exp(−kt) or a·exp(k(t−2000))+c. None derive from dome geometry. The dome curve-fits globe data, then claims the fit confirms the dome.

5. AXISYMMETRIC CONTRADICTION IN INTER-CELL INTERPRETATION: wins.html interprets TDC's extreme decay rate (−79.7 nT/yr, fastest of the five stations) as evidence that 'decay at the inter-cell boundary region exceeds decay at either lobe node, consistent with aetheric field separation geometry.' This interpretation requires longitude-dependent field structure: the inter-cell boundary is at a specific longitude between the South American (~60°W) and African (~30°E) cells. But the dome's field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) depends only on radial distance r — it is axisymmetric and predicts identical values at all longitudes for a given r. The dome cannot produce an 'inter-cell boundary' because it has no azimuthal (φ) dependence. The two-cell morphology and boundary location are outputs of spherical harmonic decomposition (tesseral harmonics with m≠0), which the dome's geometry does not support. The dome's own interpretation of its strongest station proves it is importing globe spatial structure.

files_checked: inject_ai_layer.py — field_decay_rate_nT_yr=28 (hardcoded core parameter, line 205). No WIN-041 reference. SAA separation parameters present but no multi-station aggregation logic.

monitor.py — Domain 8 ('SAA Decay Rate TTB'): formula 'B(r_SAA) exponential decay > 28 nT/yr', predicted=77.0, observed=77.0, tolerance_pct=20.0. Single station (Tsumeb). No multi-station domain. No WIN-041 reference.
predictions.js — WIN-041 NOT present. Only WIN-001 through WIN-026. PRED-012 references '>=28 nT/year'. No multi-station computation.
scoring.js — No WIN-041, no SAA, no decay references.
verify_predictions.py — No SAA references at all. Eclipse-only computations.

api/current/parameters.json — field_decay_rate=28 nT/yr described as 'Global network average' (mislabeled — this is a regional SAA value). saa_sa_cell_lon=300.0, saa_africa_cell_lon=0.0 (CHAOS-7 imports).

api/current/formulas.json — FORM-004 (SAA Node Separation Exponential, curve-fitted from CHAOS-7). FORM-015/048/098/106 (exponential decay models, I0·exp(−kt), empirical fits). FORM-111 ('Find SAA Nodes') references source 'independent_verification.py' which DOES NOT EXIST in repo (404).
docs/wins.html — WIN-041 present. Cites 5 stations: TDC −79.7, SHE −54.2, HER −45.3, PST −38.8, KOU. Claims inter-cell boundary interpretation.
code_search.found_in: inject_ai_layer.py line 205 (field_decay_rate_nT_yr=28, hardcoded), monitor.py Domain 8 (pred=obs identity 77.0/77.0), parameters.json (field_decay_rate=28, mislabeled 'Global network average'), wins.html (WIN-041 entry with 5 stations + inter-cell boundary claim)
code_search.notes: WIN-041 has ZERO dome-geometric derivation anywhere in the repository. The 28 nT/yr threshold is a hardcoded parameter adopted from INTERMAGNET observations and mislabeled as 'Global network average' in parameters.json. Domain 8 monitoring uses the pred=obs identity pattern (77.0/77.0). The inter-cell boundary interpretation in wins.html requires azimuthal field structure that the dome's axisymmetric H(r) equation cannot produce. FORM-111's source file (independent_verification.py) does not exist. All exponential decay models are empirical curve-fits to CHAOS-7 globe-model outputs.

cross_references: WIN-040 fingerprint: Same CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic dependency, same axisymmetric impossibility for longitude-specific claims
WIN-004 fingerprint: SAA separation also curve-fitted from CHAOS-7 with no dome derivation
WIN-005 fingerprint: Azimuthal symmetry breaking — identical structural argument applies to WIN-041's inter-cell boundary claim
WIN-037: Same 28 nT/yr threshold, same INTERMAGNET data source, Domain 8 monitors this WIN not WIN-041
WIN-020 fingerprint: Phantom source files — dome_predictor_interactive.py and v19_pipeline.py also don't exist, same pattern as independent_verification.py
suggested_text: The dome's monitoring code (Domain 8 in monitor.py) checks SAA decay using a pred=obs identity pattern — predicted and observed are both set to 77.0 nT/yr, making the check trivially self-confirming. The 28 nT/yr threshold is mislabeled as a 'Global network average' in the dome's parameter database, when in fact the global mean secular variation is far lower (~5–10 nT/yr); 28 nT/yr is an SAA-regional value adopted from INTERMAGNET observations. Most revealing, the dome's interpretation of TDC's extreme decay rate (−79.7 nT/yr) as evidence of 'inter-cell boundary' decay requires longitude-dependent field structure — but the dome's own field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) is axisymmetric, predicting identical values at all longitudes. The dome cannot model the two-cell morphology it claims to explain; that structure comes from spherical harmonic decomposition (tesseral harmonics with m≠0), a fundamentally globe-dependent analysis.
